title: Staelia thymbroides
intro: Staelia thymbroides, also known as Brazilian thyme or Staelia, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family. Traditionally, Staelia thymbroides has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Staelia thymbroides include terpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Staelia thymbroides is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, which are consumed to relieve respiratory issues, as well as to make topical preparations for skin and wound care.
taxonomy: Staelia thymbroides is a species of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is native to South America and is a member of the Staelia genus. The Staelia genus is part of the tribe Mentheae, which includes other mint family plants. Staelia thymbroides is a small plant with blue flowers, and it is closely related to other species within the tribe Mentheae.
